methods.description: This method calculates the limits of pollen seasons using the 90% method, which defines the start and end of a season based on cumulative pollen counts.

steps.description: The limits of pollen seasons were calculated using the 90% method whereby a season starts when 5% of the total catch has been achieved and ends when 95% has been reached.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen season limits using 90% method
outputs: pollen season start and end dates

methods.description: This method involves analyzing time-series data to understand second-order properties such as mean, variance, and serial correlation, and to model autocorrelation and cross-correlation functions.

steps.description: Data of all years and particular site have been combined into a single table, in which X was the cumulative number of a day from 1 January 2001, and Y-the value of daily concentrations of taxon A in site B.
steps.searchable_summary: combine yearly data into a single table with cumulative days and daily concentrations

steps.description: Second-order properties, such as the mean, variance, and serial correlation, play a key role in the study of time-series data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze second-order properties of time-series data

steps.description: The autocovariance function (acvf), c k , can be defined as a function of the lag k.
steps.searchable_summary: define autocovariance function based on lag

steps.description: The estimate of autocovariance c k is defined as: where x is the sample mean of the time series.
The most satisfactory estimate of the kth lag autocorrelation q k is calculated.
A plot of the correlation function against the lag is referred to as a correlogram.
steps.searchable_summary: plot correlogram of correlation function against lag

steps.description: The cross-correlation function, in contrast to the autocorrelation function, is not symmetric.
The ccf function can be shown as a plot of r k ðx; yÞ against k, which is called the cross-correlogram.
steps.searchable_summary: plot cross-correlogram of ccf function against lag

steps.description: The calculation of 61 correlograms and 84 cross-correlograms as well as their mathematical modelling were performed for up to 25 days back.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and model correlograms and cross-correlograms

steps.description: Sample correlograms were fitted to the parametric form by adjusting model parameters: the sill, nugget, and range.
steps.searchable_summary: fit correlograms to parametric model by adjusting parameters

steps.description: The autocorrelation analysis was conducted twice: for the entire set and with the extremes eliminated.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct autocorrelation analysis with and without extremes

steps.description: The Pearson's coefficient of product-moment correlation between all the cross-correlograms was calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson's correlation coefficient for cross-correlograms

steps.description: A hierarchical cluster analysis using Ward's method was carried out.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hierarchical cluster analysis using Ward's method
